rules: NEVER install or add runtime npm dependencies without explicit user approval. If a solution requires a new package, propose it and wait for confirmation before running npm install.
NEVER edit files inside dist/ directly. The dist/ directory is generated by `npm run build` and must not be manually modified.
After adding a new component or hook, ALWAYS verify it is exported from src/components/index.ts before finishing.
When changing a public component's props interface, update the corresponding story's argTypes and the docs.description.component props table to match.
NEVER use @ts-ignore or @ts-expect-error without adding an inline comment explaining exactly why it is necessary.

All interactive components (buttons, inputs, toggles, selects, tabs, modals) MUST have keyboard event handlers and appropriate ARIA attributes. Do not ship interactive components without accessibility.
Use only Tailwind v4 utility classes and the existing CSS variable tokens for styling. Do not introduce inline styles, new CSS files, or external styling libraries.
Responsive size props must use ResponsiveValue<T> from src/utils/responsive.ts. Never hardcode breakpoint-specific classes directly in component JSX.
Run `npm run lint && npm run type-check` after any change to a component, hook, or utility file. Do not present work as done until both pass.

Stories are living documentation. Treat a component without stories as incomplete. Every public component needs: Default, variant coverage, size coverage, disabled state, and an AllVariants render story.
Cyberpunk identity: use thematic copy in stories and docs (e.g. 'Execute Protocol', 'Neural Sync', 'System Override') — not generic placeholder text like 'Click me' or 'Label'.
Keep diffs minimal and focused. Do not reformat unrelated code, add comments to unchanged lines, or refactor outside the scope of the current task.
Before changing a public API (component props, hook signatures, exported types), check CHANGELOG.md and confirm the change is intentional — it may require a semver bump.
